framework.name: Agent Policy Specification (WS-Policy/WS-Security/OAuth2 Integration)
framework.description: Proposed unified policy framework for AI agent authorization combining WS-Policy (for declaring requirements), WS-Security (for message-level security: signing, encryption, tokens), and OAuth 2.0 (for delegated authorization flows). Creates three-layer architecture: policy advertisement → negotiation → runtime enforcement, ensuring agents only act under explicit human authorization with cryptographic proof.
framework.raw_quote: By combining them, we envision an Agent Policy spec that allows agents to negotiate trust and authority at the start of any interaction. WS-Policy — for expressing what an agent requires or capable of in terms of security... WS-Security — for the low-level mechanics of signing, encrypting, and attaching tokens to messages... OAuth 2.0 — for delegated authorization flows, so agents can obtain and use tokens on behalf of users, under user consent.
